evidence_summary: Establish the surreal temporal rules of the tea party through a physical object reveal. The scene must ground the abstract concept of Time into a tangible, mechanical conflict between characters.

The Hatter reveals his watch, which tracks the day but not the hour. He explains that because he quarreled with "Time," it is perpetually tea-time. This explanation establishes the surrealist rules governing their constant movement around the table.
From intellectual debate to a sense of temporal absurdity.
The group is engaged in an intellectual debate around the tea table.
The reality of perpetual tea-time is established through the revealed watch.
The Hatter presents his watch to explain why it is always tea-time.
